title: DashPass Growth Drives Up DoorDash Revenue
text: DashPass subscription membership growth has become a key driver of DoorDash's revenue and profitability expansion. The membership program achieved record engagement and accelerated member growth in recent periods, signaling heightened stickiness and retention among subscribers. Year-over-year growth in U.S. DashPass members accelerated in Q1 2026, driven by strong signup growth and reduced churn rates. DashPass members demonstrate significantly higher order frequency compared to non-members, with average order frequency reaching all-time highs in Q2 2025. The company explicitly highlighted DashPass as emblematic of its strategy to maximize long-term profit dollar production. While DashPass orders often generate lower contribution margin per order than non-DashPass orders on average, the members have higher retention and order frequency, supporting faster growth and higher long-term profit dollars. Membership expansion supports retention and profit dollars even if margin per order compresses modestly, as subscribers order more frequently. More than 35 million members across DashPass, Wolt Plus, and Deliveroo Plus exited 2025, representing substantial recurring revenue base. The strong membership trends validate DoorDash's strategy of converting occasional users into loyal, high-frequency subscribers, driving both top-line growth and lifetime value improvement.
